admission_type: free access with verification of curricular requirements and assessment of personal readiness (interview, possible preliminary quiz)

academic_requirements.summary: Free-access master's degree (120 CFU, 2 years) requiring a Bachelor degree, adequate disciplinary background, an assessment of personal readiness by interview, and a recognised B2 English certificate. Pre-enrolment and the readiness assessment are free of charge.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A Bachelor's degree (laurea) of at least 180 CFU/ECTS, or an equivalent qualification.
academic_requirements.curricular_credits: Computer Science is the natural feeder; students from other areas of Science and Engineering are welcome provided they have at least 50% of credits in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Mathematics, Physics or Statistics.
academic_requirements.adequacy_of_preparation: Adequacy of basic knowledge in computer science and mathematics is assessed during the admission procedure by interview, to which a preliminary quiz may be added.
academic_requirements.minimum_cgpa_non_eu: [uncertain] For non-EU pre-evaluation on UniGeApply, a minimum CGPA around 75% of the maximum obtainable has been indicated; confirm against the live UniGeApply listing.
academic_requirements.two_tracks: Two tracks are offered: Data Science & Engineering and Software Security & Engineering.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Procedures differ by applicant nationality and degree origin (Italian vs foreign university); non-EU candidates abroad are assessed during UniGeApply pre-evaluation.

accepted_proofs: From A.Y. 2025/26 onward, a valid B2 (or higher) certificate among those recognised by the University (list at https://clat.unige.it/en/CertificazioniRiconosciute) is required.
For international applicants, a degree taught in English / medium-of-instruction certificate is also accepted; an English verification interview was previously offered (A.Y. 2024/25).
exemption_rules: [uncertain] English-requirement wording varied between A.Y. 2024/25 (certificate OR verification interview) and 2025/26 (valid certificate required); the exact 2026/27 wording should be reconfirmed. Accepted tests (IELTS/TOEFL) and minimum scores are defined on the CLAT recognised-certificates list.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English level is verified during the admission procedure; international applicants and those abroad are assessed during pre-evaluation on UniGeApply, others in person at Via Dodecaneso 35, Genoa.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Assessment of personal readiness: a test in mathematics and computer science by interview, to which a preliminary quiz may be added. NOT a TOLC.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants to the Computer Science master.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Interview assessing basic specialist knowledge in mathematics and computer science needed to succeed in the programme; a preliminary quiz may be added. For international applicants/those abroad it is conducted remotely during UniGeApply pre-evaluation.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: [uncertain] No numerical pass threshold for the interview is published on the page; adequacy is judged by the board, and the assessment is free of charge.
entry_exam_or_test.location: International applicants/those abroad are assessed during UniGeApply pre-evaluation; other students are assessed in person at Via Dodecaneso 35, Genoa, and notified by email.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; English-taught master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Open-access master pre-registration on the UniGe calendar: from 9 April to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment (first year): from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment. Enrolment in years after the first: from 15 July to 5 October 2026, with arrears from 6 October to 5 November 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: The programme deadlines page lists uniform open-access enrolment dates and does not separate EU vs non-EU first-year windows.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After UniGeApply eligibility, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and apply for a study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: [uncertain] No programme-specific early closure is published for the Computer Science master; the only listed fall early closures are Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology (master, 13 Feb 2026), Maritime Science and Technology (bachelor, 18 Feb 2026) and Computer Engineering (bachelor, 8 Mar 2026).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session via UniGeApply; the A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has now closed (closed 20 March 2026).

required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply (max 2 MB per document).
If still in progress, the degree must be completed by 15 August 2026.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records (exams with credits and grades)
required_documents.notes: Used to verify the disciplinary credit background (at least 50% credits in CS/CE/Math/Physics/Statistics).

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2 or higher) or medium-of-instruction certificate
required_documents.notes: From 2025/26 a valid recognised B2-or-higher certificate is required (CLAT recognised list); a degree taught in English is also accepted for international applicants.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport (non-EU; only identity document accepted) and visa/pre-enrolment documents via Universitaly
required_documents.notes: Photo as JPG, supporting documents as PDF up to 2 MB on UniGeApply; foreign qualifications require Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability at enrolment.

source_quotes.quote: COMPUTER SCIENCE, Master's Degree (D.M. 1649/2023), Class LM-18, Teaching language English, Genova (Via Dodecaneso, 35), 120 CFU, 2 years.
we welcome students from different areas of Science and Engineering provided that they have at least 50% credits in: Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Math, Physics, Statistics
Adequacy of basic knowledge in computer science and mathematics will be assessed during the admission procedure by means of an interview, to which a preliminary quiz may be added.
For 2025/26 onward, applicants must present a valid B2 (or higher) certificate among those listed.
Pre-enrolment and the assessment of the adequacy of personal preparation are free of charge.
Spring session opens 9 April 2026; Master's degrees close 28 August 2026. Fall session (non-EU visa applicants) opens 26 November 2025, closes 20 March 2026.

uncertainty_notes: Pilot already confirmed English; re-fetched in the mandatory rich object/array structure (the previous flat-string file was replaced).
No numerical pass threshold for the mathematics/computer-science interview is published; adequacy is judged by the assessment board.
A 75% CGPA minimum referenced for non-EU pre-evaluation comes from the UniGeApply summary and should be confirmed against the live UniGeApply listing.
English-requirement wording varied between A.Y. 2024/25 and 2025/26; confirm exact 2026/27 wording. Accepted certificate types/scores are on the CLAT recognised list.
The programme deadlines page gives the generic open-access enrolment calendar and does not separate EU vs non-EU first-year application windows; those come from the institution-wide UniGeApply pages.
